Nano Express 5 (2024) 035008: magniﬁcations are presented in ﬁgure 7. The FE-SEM images of the synthesized nanoparticles provide clear
evidence of the production of smooth and spherical particles. Figure 7 illustrates that upon encapsulation of
compounds 9c and 9e into PLGA NPs, the NPs maintained their spherical shape and exhibited a nearly uniform
size distribution. The average particle size derived from the particle size distribution histogram depicted on the
FE-SEM micrograph was 140.6 nm for empty NP, 172.4 nm for 9c/NP, and 181.2 nm for 9e/NP. In contrast to
the dynamic light scattering technique, the microscopic methods yield smaller particle diameters. The particle
diameters obtained from the microscopic methods are directly related to measuring the particle diameter by
evaporating the water in the nanoparticle suspension before microscopic analysis, in contrast to the dynamic
light scattering method. The hydrodynamic diameter measured through dynamic light scattering represents the
diameter of particles dispersed in an aqueous medium, encompassing the oppositely charged ion layer
surrounding the particle surface within the medium. The particles in the aqueous buffer exhibit increased sizes
in DLS measurements due to hydration and swelling, in contrast to FE-SEM.
4. Conclusions
As a result, the synthesis of bicyclic heterocyclic compounds containing nitrogen atoms such as tetrazine,
triazole, and coumarin has been reported. The antioxidant activities of these compounds were compared at a
constant concentration using the DPPH method, and nanoparticle encapsulation was carried out for molecules
with high activity. The results obtained indicate that among the nine synthesized coumarin derivatives,
compounds 9c and 9e exhibit the highest antioxidant activity due to their ability to donate H radicals.
Furthermore, the resonance stability in molecule 9e facilitates easier breakdown of the allylic radical hydrogen,
enhancing its antioxidant activity by increasing its propensity to donate hydrogen radicals. Therefore, the
ﬁndings of this study suggest the development of a promising platform for therapeutic applications related to
oxidative stress and may contribute to future research in this area.
